channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Protesters take to the Texas Capitol after ICE operations in Austin
item.description: On Sunday afternoon, a crowd of protestors gathered at the Texas Capitol after U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement raids were conducted in Austin.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Torres, NewsNation

item.title: Rain in Southern California creates mudflows but helps firefighters
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — More rain fell Monday on parts of Southern California after causing mudflows over the weekend, helping firefighters but boosting the risk of toxic ash runoff in areas scorched by Los Angeles-area wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Auschwitz memorial holds observances on the 80th anniversary of the death camp's liberation
item.description: OSWIECIM, Poland (AP) — The 80th anniversary of the liberation of Auschwitz by Soviet troops is being marked on Monday at the site of the former death camp, a ceremony that is widely being treated as the last major observance that any notable number of survivors will be able to attend.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump's visit to small-town North Carolina brings hope to hurricane survivors who lost everything
item.description: North Carolina locals speak with Fox News Digital about their hopes that the Trump administration will bring more assistance to those who lost everything in Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Auschwitz memorial marks 80th anniversary of the death camp's liberation
item.description: Nazi German forces murdered some 1.1 million people at the site in southern Poland, which was under German occupation during World War II.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: International Holocaust Remembrance Day: Survivor recounts how mother saved her life, reveals message to UN
item.description: Holocaust survivor Marianne Miller tells Fox News Digital the message she will share while speaking at the U.N. for International Holocaust Remembrance Day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House says Colombia will take deported migrants after Trump showdown
item.description: The White House said Sunday night that a U.S.-Colombia agreement had come together in the wake of a back-and-forth between the two countries over topics including immigration and tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: Repeat sex trafficker in Mass. sentenced to 13 years in prison
item.description: A Stoughton, Massachusetts man who has prior convictions of sex trafficking has been sentenced to 13 years in prison for sex trafficking multiple adult women.
item.dc:creator: Beck Wehner, NewsNation

item.title: Haitian migrants in Springfield, Ohio, turn to faith amid deportation fears
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Ohio (AP) — At the end of his Sunday service, the pastor of the First Haitian Evangelical Church of Springfield, Ohio, asked ushers and musicians to form a circle around him as he knelt in prayer, flanked by the flags of Haiti and the United States.
item.dc:creator: LUIS ANDRES HENAO and JESSIE WARDARSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Arrest quotas for ICE officers 'a bad idea': William Enyart
item.description: Enacting a daily arrest quota for ICE agents would be "asking for an abuse of their police powers," Ret. U.S. Army Major Gen. William Enyart said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Ohio State celebrates national championship run amid change and farewells
item.description: Fans braved 30-degree temperatures to welcome home a team that beat five top-10 rivals on its way to the title, including Oregon in the Rose Bowl, Texas in the Cotton Bowl and Notre Dame in the College Football Playoff championship game.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE KRAFT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Romanian prehistoric gold treasures stolen from Dutch museum
item.description: The Geto-Dacian artifacts belonged to the National History Museum in the capital Bucharest and were on loan to the Drents Museum in the Netherlands.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Sen. Marshall 'absolutely' comfortable with Trump’s purge of inspectors general
item.description: Sen. Roger Marshall (R-Kan.) suggested that, like Reagan, Trump could potentially rehire some of the watchdog officials after cleaning house at the agencies.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: DEA busts drug traffickers in makeshift nightclub, takes 50 illegal aliens into custody
item.description: Federal agents busted alleged traffickers at a Colorado makeshift nightclub for Tren de Aragua affiliates, taking nearly 50 illegal immigrants into custody.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Researchers studying whether medical cannabis could help late-stage dementia patients
item.description: As research continues, those involved say this would be a game changer, especially for patients with very limited options.
item.dc:creator: Josh Shortt, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: In policy reversal, Trump eliminates help for Black and Latino communities hit harder by pollution
item.description: For four years, the Environmental Protection Agency made environmental justice one of its biggest priorities, working to improve health conditions in heavily-polluted communities often made up largely of Black, Latino and low-income Americans. Now that short-lived era is over.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PHILLIS and ALEXA ST. JOHN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Acting deputy AG head visits Chicago to 'observe' immigration enforcement
item.description: Chicago residents, especially in immigrant circles, have already been on edge for months in anticipation of large-scale immigration arrests promised by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Hamas frees 4 female Israeli soldiers in exchange for 200 Palestinian prisoners as ceasefire holds
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Four female Israeli soldiers who were taken in the attack that sparked the war in Gaza returned to Israel on Saturday after Hamas militants paraded them before a crowd of thousands in Gaza City and handed them over to the Red Cross.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE, SAM MEDNICK, WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Ozempic and similar weight loss drugs may lower risk of 42 health conditions, but also pose risks
item.description: The Food and Drug Administration’s approval of Ozempic in 2021 for weight loss treatment ushered in a new era for the class of drugs called glucagon-like peptide-1 agonists, or GLP-1.
item.dc:creator: Ziyad Al-Aly Clinical Epidemiologist, Washington University in St. Louis, KTLA 5

item.title: Your wages will stretch the furthest in these major US metros, study finds
item.description: A new study by finance site Bankrate found that, after adjusting for the cost of living, the average worker's buying power only grew (compared to the other cities) in a handful of large metros.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: Oklahoma bill would make it a crime punishable by jail time to "possess" a shopping cart belonging to a business
item.description: An Oklahoma lawmaker is proposing a law that would make it a crime punishable by up to a year in jail for anyone caught “in possession of” a shopping cart taken from a store.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Humphrey/KFOR, WGN 9

item.title: Inflation isn't the only reason egg prices are soaring
item.description: Bird flu has killed millions of chickens on U.S. farms, causing a spike in the cost of eggs for consumers.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bass, NewsNation

item.title: Live updates: Trump visits Las Vegas after whirlwind first days in office
item.description: President Trump is in Las Vegas Saturday after a whirlwind first few days in the Oval Office, filled with executive orders and travel across the country as his agenda takes form in Washington.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, Fox 5

item.title: As schools in LA reopen, parents worry about harmful ash from wildfires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Kelli Ferrone wiped blinds and window sills inside her daughters’ elementary school, just a half mile from the devastating Palisades Fire that scorched the coastal enclave in Los Angeles. The cotton swabs and wipes came up black. There is scattered ash and soot at her feet.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mexico refuses Trump admin. migrant deportation flights, sources say
item.description: U.S. military aircraft carried out two similar flights, each with about 80 migrants, to Guatemala on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Aurora borealis returns: When and where to watch the northern lights
item.description: Sky gazers in several U.S. states could get a colorful glimpse of the northern lights as we enter the weekend, thanks to a recent geomagnetic storm.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KTLA 5

item.title: Vance breaks tie to confirm Pete Hegseth as Defense secretary
item.description: Vice President Vance on Friday broke a Senate tie to confirm Pete Hegseth as President Trump's secretary of Defense, capping a bruising two-month fight over the nominee, who faced a litany of allegations that included sexual misconduct, financial mismanagement and excessive drinking. GOP Sens.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WGN 9

item.title: Trump arrives in Los Angeles to tour wildfire damage
item.description: Prior to arriving in California Friday, Trump told reporters in North Carolina that the Federal Government would not provide aid to Los Angeles recovery efforts unless new voter ID laws were passed and changes were made to the state's water management system.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, WGN 9

item.title: Trump cuts Dr. Fauci's security detail
item.description: Asked if he would feel partially responsible if something were to happen to Fauci, Trump said he would not.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Oregon couple who posed as firefighters during L.A. wildfires charged, face potential jail time
item.description: The Oregon couple who allegedly tried to sneak into a Palisades Fire evacuation zone while impersonating firefighters are facing charges and potential jail time, according to prosecutors.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KRON4

item.title: Trump cites voter ID, water flow as conditions for LA wildfire aid
item.description: President Donald Trump on Friday said he wanted to see two actions taken in California before he offered federal support for Los Angeles as it grapples with wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: Spirit Airlines updates dress code, tattoo policy for passengers
item.description: Passengers flying on Spirit Airlines can now be removed from a flight if a tattoo or other "body art" is deemed "lewd, obscene, or offensive.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, Fox 5

item.title: Flame thrower and 'flusher' truck: Southern cities get creative to clear snow, ice
item.description: Days after a winter storm dropped ice and record-breaking snow, cleanup efforts were underway Thursday in several major Southern cities such as New Orleans, where crews were removing snow the same way they remove trash, drink cups and plastic beads after Mardi Gras.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump targets California water policy as he prepares to tour LA fire damage
item.description: As President Donald Trump prepares to tour wildfire damage in California, he's zeroing in on one of his frequent targets for criticism: State water policy. Since the fires broke out Jan.
item.dc:creator: AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, ABC 4
